title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
An automated funds transfer (AFT) is a debit or credit transaction. Debit transactions are known as pre-authorized debits (PADs). Credit transactions are known as direct deposits.
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Re-presentment
An AFT Re-presentment is an AFT debit transaction that has been returned for the return reason code 901 芒聙聯 NSF, or code 908 芒聙聯 Funds Not Cleared, and is re-submitted on a one-time only basis.
Branch Number
A branch number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The branch number, in conjunction with the 3-digit institution number, makes up the transit number.
Business Day
A business day is a weekday including Monday to Friday except for statutory holidays that fall on or are observed on a weekday.
Business PAD
A PAD drawn on the account of a payor for the payment of goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the payor including, but not limited to, payments between franchisees and franchisers, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
CPA
CPA means the Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Credit Transaction
A credit transaction is a credit payment item exchanged in an AFT file as defined in CPA Standard 005. Also known as a Direct Deposit.
Debit Transaction
A debit transaction is a pre-authorized debit within the meaning of Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, exchanged in an AFT file as defined in CPA Standard 005.
Direct Clearer
A direct clearer is a member of the CPA that meets the requirements of the association and settles for clearing items through a settlement account at the Bank of Canada.
Direct Deposit
A direct deposit is an AFT credit transaction such as a payroll deposit.
Due Date
The due date is the date on which the payor is to be debited or the date on which the payee is to receive payment.
Error Correction
An error correction is an AFT transaction used to correct an error made by the originator or the originating direct clearer. An error correction corrects a transaction(s) posted to the recipient芒聙聶s account(s) in error. Error corrections apply to both transactions and AFT files.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
File Creation Number
A file creation number is a consecutive number automatically assigned to an AFT file during preparation for transmission. Central 1 uses the file creation number for verification purposes because files must be received in sequential order.
File Error Correction
A file error correction is an AFT transaction that is sent to the processing direct clearers to correct transactions that were posted to the recipients芒聙聶 accounts in error. A file error correction transaction is generated for each transaction in the original AFT file. A file error correction is sometimes referred to as a Mass Error Correction or a File Reversal.
File Recall
A file recall is when an originator requests the return of an AFT file, which has not yet been processed at Central 1. See Recall.
Member Payee
A member payee is a financial institution that is also a payee.
Notice of Change (NOC)
A notice of change (NOC) is a non-financial transaction created by the receiving financial institution. It informs the payment originator of changes to the payment routing information.
Offsetting Transaction
The offsetting transaction is the debit or credit transaction posted to the originator芒聙聶s account that equals the total value of the individual debit or credit transactions within an outgoing AFT file. The offsetting transaction is also a transaction that offsets an AFT item returned through the Online Return System (ORS) or the Batch Return Service if the software does not create the offsetting entry. An offsetting transaction can also be referred to as an Offsetting Entry.
ORS
The Online Return System (ORS) is a web-based system that facilitates the return of AFT transactions and the creation of AFT notice of change transactions.
Originating Direct Clearer
The originating direct clearer delivers AFT files to another direct clearer. Central 1 is the originating direct clearer for financial institutions in BC and Ontario.
Originator
An originator is the financial institution or business member that creates an AFT file.
Originator ID
An Originator ID is the 10-digit number assigned by Central 1 that identifies the originator of the AFT file.
Payee
The payee is the entity for which the credit transaction is intended.
PaymentStream
AFT
PaymentStream
AFT allows users to create AFT transactions and upload AFT files.
Payor
The payor is the entity for which the debit transaction is intended.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A pre-authorized debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a payee or member payee that is drawn on an account of a payor (for example, mortgage payment, strata payment). See Debit Transaction.
Pre-authorized Payment (PAP)
See AFT Transaction.
Processing Direct Clearer
The processing direct clearer is the clearer that processes AFT transactions received from an originating direct clearer.
Recall
A recall is a request to return an AFT credit transaction that has not been credited to the payee芒聙聶s account. A recall applies to individual credit transactions and to AFT files. Recalled AFT files can contain both credits and debits. Individual debits cannot be recalled. See Transaction Recall and File Recall.
Receiving Financial Institution
The receiving financial institution is the financial institution that receives AFT transactions from another financial institution.
Reject
A reject is a transaction in the AFT file that cannot be processed by Central 1, the other data centre, or the receiving financial institution.
Run
A run is the Central 1 processing period. In BC, Canadian AFT transactions are processed up to four runs each calendar day and USD AFT Transactions are processed in one run each business day. In Ontario, Canadian AFT transactions are processed up to three runs each calendar day and USD AFT transactions are processed in one run each business day.
Settlement Date
The settlement date is the date on which Central 1 posts a transaction to the financial institution. This is usually the due date.
Transit Number
The transit number consists of the branch Number (five digits) plus the institution number (three digits). Each financial institution has a unique transit number (e.g., 80912010).
Transaction Recall
See Recall.
US AFT Transaction
A US dollar AFT transaction is a transaction between two Canadian financial institutions in US dollars.
END
